texto: History is for human self-knowledge…the only clue to what man can do is what man has done. The value of history, then, is that it teaches us what man has done and thus what man is.
explicacao: Citação de R.G. Collingwood sobre temas ligados a sabedoria. “History is for human self-knowledge…the only clue to what man can do is what man has done. The va…” articula sabedoria, history, self-knowledge em registro de sabedoria, com relevância para contextos como self-knowledge.

frase_original: History is for human self-knowledge…the only clue to what man can do is what man has done. The value of history, then, is that it teaches us what man has done and thus what man is.

texto: Every kind of language is... specialized form of bodily gesture, and in this sense it may be said that the dance is the mother of all languages... an original language of total bodily gesture.This "original" language of total bodily gesture is thus the one and only real language, which everybody who is in any way expressing himself is using all the time. What we call speech and the other kinds of language are only parts of it which have undergone specialized development.
explicacao: Orientação direta de R.G. Collingwood sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. A afirmação “Every kind of language is... specialized form of bodily gesture, and in this sense it may be said…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a familia, anthropology, body-language, útil em momentos de body-language e dance.
